channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Family of missing Phoenix truck driver continues search after Cape Town floods
item.description: The search for Poovin Narainsamy, a Phoenix truck driver aged 40, is ongoing after his Scania R580 was swept away by floodwaters in the R46 Wolseley area near Cape Town last week. Narainsamy was trans...

item.title: US Introduces Policy Requiring Most Green Card Applicants to Apply from Abroad
item.description: WASHINGTON: The US Citizenship and Immigration Services (USCIS) on Friday unveiled a significant policy shift affecting foreigners seeking permanent residency in America. Under this new directive, mos...

item.title: Victorian Outdoor Portraits Showcase 19th Century Family Life and Fashion
item.description: In the mid-to-late 19th century, Victorian outdoor portraits became a fashionable alternative to traditional studio photography. Unlike indoor sessions that relied on heavy curtains and painted backdr...

item.title: WeBuyCars expands third-party vehicle integration and digital inspection tools to boost growth
item.description: WeBuyCars is steadily broadening its operations by integrating vehicles sourced from third parties, including banks, rental fleets, and dealerships, into its network of dealerships. Over the past two ...

item.title: Rosalía urges greater diversity in the music industry
item.description: During her acceptance speech for the International Songwriter of the Year Award at the Ivor Novellos in London, Spanish singer Rosalía highlighted the lack of diversity in the music industry. She emph...

item.title: Tanzania Enhances Public Service Policies for Disability Inclusion
item.description: Tanzania government imetoka mbele kuimarisha workplace protections na kuunda environment yenye inclusivity kwa public servants wenye disabilities. Deputy Minister wa President’s Office for Public Serv...

item.title: Limpopo traffic officers intercept minibuses carrying undocumented Zimbabwean nationals, drivers arrested
item.description: On Friday morning, traffic officers in Limpopo intercepted two minibuses near Polokwane that were transporting a total of 48 undocumented Zimbabwean nationals. The vehicles were stopped during a routi...

item.title: Colorado Governor Jared Polis Commutes Sentence of Election Denier, Sparking Political Backlash
item.description: Colorado Governor Jared Polis has commuted the sentence of Tina Peters, a former county clerk convicted of election-related crimes. Peters, who has publicly denied election results, praised Polis for ...

item.title: Explosion at Staten Island Shipyard Injures Dozens and Claims One Life
item.description: A significant explosion occurred at a shipyard in Mariners Harbor, Staten Island, New York City, on Friday afternoon, resulting in at least one civilian death and 37 injuries, including firefighters a...

item.title: Blue Jays defeat Pirates 6-2 with strong performances from Pinango, Springer, and Gausman
item.description: The Toronto Blue Jays secured a 6-2 victory over the Pittsburgh Pirates on Friday night, highlighted by standout performances from Yohendrick Pinango, George Springer, and starting pitcher Kevin Gausm...

item.title: Daniel Etim-Effiong Advises on Handling Cheating in Marriage With Care
item.description: Nollywood actor and filmmaker Daniel Etim-Effiong don yan say people suppose handle cheating inside marriage with understanding, no just anger. For one interview with Ifedayo Agoro, Daniel talk say wh...

item.title: Emerson Fittipaldi Becomes First Driver to Win Over $1 Million at the 1989 Indianapolis 500
item.description: In a historic moment at the 1989 Indianapolis 500, Emerson Fittipaldi made headlines by becoming the first driver to earn over one million dollars in prize money, totaling $1,001,604. Instead of recei...

item.title: Pakistan mediates to end Iran-U.S. conflict amid regional tensions
item.description: The ongoing conflict between Iran and Israel, with U.S. involvement, continues to destabilise West Asia, prompting Pakistan to step in as a mediator. On May 22, 2026, Pakistan Army Chief Field Marshal...

item.title: Mohamed Salah to Leave Liverpool Following Managerial Disputes
item.description: Mohamed Salah has confirmed that he will depart Liverpool at the end of the season after nearly a decade at the club. His decision comes following a series of disagreements with manager Arne Slot and ...

item.title: UK set for record-breaking May temperatures over bank holiday weekend
item.description: The UK is experiencing unusually high temperatures for May, which are expected to intensify over the upcoming bank holiday weekend. Daytime highs are predicted to reach the upper 20s Celsius across mu...

item.title: Canada receives updates on Ebola and hantavirus; Danielle Smith addresses national confidence
item.description: On May 22, 2026, Canadians received updates regarding public health concerns, specifically Ebola and hantavirus. Dr. Joss Reimer, Canada's chief public health officer, emphasised that while the outbre...

item.title: Decline in paid parental leave pushes new parents to rely on UIF
item.description: Parental leave benefits in South Africa's private sector are declining, which may force new parents to rely on the Unemployment Insurance Fund (UIF) to supplement income while on leave. Research from ...

item.title: Cachalia Calls for Stronger Measures on Alcohol-Linked Violence in South Africa
item.description: Acting Police Minister Professor Firoz Cachalia released the crime statistics for the fourth quarter of the 2025/26 financial year, highlighting the ongoing impact of alcohol on violent crime. While o...

item.title: Victoria revises municipal election polling locations to enhance voter access and traffic flow
item.description: The City of Victoria has announced updates to its polling plan ahead of the upcoming municipal election, aiming to improve voter accessibility and streamline the flow at voting stations. The changes i...

item.title: US government publishes additional declassified UFO reports
item.description: The United States Defence Department has made public a second batch of previously classified documents detailing alleged sightings of unidentified flying objects, or UFOs. These files include accounts...
